description: Nested-structure canonicalization cases. Object key sort order (UCS-2 code-unit order per RFC 8785) is the single most common cross-implementation divergence point.

cases.name: reverse alphabetical
cases.rationale: keys MUST be sorted in UCS-2 code-unit order for canonical output

cases.name: case sensitive sort
cases.rationale: UCS-2 sort: uppercase A (0x41) before uppercase B (0x42) before lowercase a (0x61) before lowercase b (0x62). Common divergence point for libraries using locale-aware sort

cases.name: digit and letter keys
cases.rationale: digits (0x30+) sort before letters (0x41+/0x61+) in UCS-2

cases.name: unicode keys
cases.rationale: ASCII sorts before BMP Unicode (0x65E5 for 日 is above 0x7A for z)

cases.name: nested object
cases.rationale: recursive sort: inner object keys sorted too

cases.name: array of objects
cases.rationale: each object within array is sorted independently; array order is preserved

cases.name: mixed types nested
cases.rationale: null, boolean, array values canonicalize correctly; keys still sort

cases.name: deeply nested
cases.rationale: no arbitrary depth limit; test guards against stack-based canonicalization bugs

cases.name: duplicate keys (forbidden input)
cases.rationale: PARSING CONCERN, not canonicalization: RFC 8785 §3.2.3 requires input JSON to have unique keys. Implementations SHOULD reject duplicate keys at parse time. This case documents the requirement; actual test would provide malformed input at the string level
